layman_headline: Fear dominates the crypto market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin around $64K and most altcoins bleeding. While Ethereum shows some promising short-term chart patterns like 'Three White Soldiers' (three strong up-candles in a row), Bitcoin's high dominance at 56.5% means money is flowing away from altcoins. None of the candidates present a compelling enough setup with clear risk/reward to justify opening a new position today.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait for a clearer setup. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,800-$1,820 range with RSI dropping below 40, that could be a better entry point. Don't chase anything in this fearful market.
expert_reasoning: Systematic evaluation of all 10 candidates reveals no high-conviction entry. ETH is the best prospect: RSI at 44 is neutral-to-leaning-oversold, Three White Soldiers pattern on chart is bullish, and R/R of 2.55x to resistance at $2,000 vs support at $1,900 is acceptable. However, the current price at $1,858 is already below the chart's noted $1,928 level, suggesting a gap-down that invalidates some of the bullish pattern. BTC dominance at 56.5% is a headwind for all altcoin plays. Fear & Greed at 28 with a declining trend (31→28 in one day) suggests further downside risk before a reversal. Per validated rules, with limited signal availability across candidates (no funding rates, no MACD crosses, incomplete SMA data), confidence should be capped at 30%.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising could continue draining altcoin liquidity for weeks
Fear & Greed declining from 31 to 28 suggests sentiment hasn't bottomed — could cascade to Extreme Fear territory
EU sanctions on $120B crypto network could trigger compliance-driven selling pressure across exchanges
watch_list: Fear & Greed Index — if it drops below 25 into Extreme Fear, expect further downside before any bounce
ETH price action around $1,800 support — a hold there with RSI dropping to 35-38 would create a stronger buy setup
Bitcoin options $5B cluster expiry timing — could catalyze directional move in BTC that drags alts
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear-driven pullback with BTC at $64K and high dominance squeezing altcoins. Mixed signals from bullish institutional positioning (Bitcoin options) versus bearish macro headlines (EU sanctions, mining bankruptcies) create a wait-and-see environment.
context_banner: Market fear at 28 with high BTC dominance and incomplete signals across candidates — discipline demands patience over forced entries.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) and declining from yesterday's 31 — sentiment hasn't stabilized, suggesting more downside risk before a tradeable bottom forms.
ETH RSI at 44 is the most promising among candidates but still neutral — not yet in the oversold zone (below 35) where our 83% win-rate RSI signal triggers most reliably.
ETH's Three White Soldiers pattern is bullish but price has already dropped below the pattern's reference price ($1,928 vs current $1,858), partially invalidating the signal.
EU's 21st sanctions package targeting a $120B crypto network introduces regulatory uncertainty that could pressure prices near-term despite bullish $5B Bitcoin options cluster.
Samsung Wallet adding stablecoin support and RWA growth on Hyperliquid signal institutional adoption progress, but these are medium-term catalysts not immediate price drivers.
